name: Sao Paulo State of Federative Republic of Brazil v. American Tobacco Company, Inc.
question: Should a judge be removed from a case when his name previously appeared on a brief for a case involving one of the parties?
description: A case in which the Court held that the standard for removing a judge from a case based on potential partiality is whether a reasonable person, knowing all the circumstances, would expect the judge in question to have actual knowledge of his bias or partiality.
